sections.paragraph:
κατάκειμαι impf. κατεκείμην; fut. 2 sg. κατακείσῃ Pr 23:34 (s. κεῖμαι; Hom. et al.; grave ins: Sb 6089, 1; pap, LXX, TestSol 11:2; TestReub 3:13; Joseph.; Mel., P. 80, 594)

① to be in a recumbent position free from any activity, lie down

ⓐ in a gener. sense rest (M. Ant. 5, 1, 1; TestReub; Mel.) MPol 7:1. Also of animals lying in the meadow Hs 9, 1, 9.

ⓑ of sick people (Hdt. 7, 229; Aristoph., Eccl. 313; Lucian, Icarom. 31; Plut., Cic. 43, 3; Jos., Ant. 6, 214; PRyl 68, 16 [89 b.c.]; PTebt 422, 19; TestSol) J 5:3, 6. W. the sickness given: Ac 28:8. κατέκετο πυρέσσουσα she lay sick w. a fever Mk 1:30. Also of one who has already died 5:40 v.l. W. indication of the place where: ἐπί τινος lie on someth. ἐπὶ κραβάττου Ac 9:33. ἐπί τι on someth. Lk 5:25; cp. Mk 2:4.

② to recline (on a couch) for the purpose of dining, recline for a meal, dine (X., An. 6, 1, 4, Symp. 1, 14; Pla., Symp. 177d, Rep. 2, 372d) abs. (Dio Chrys. 31 [48], 3 οἱ κατακείμενοι; Jos., Vi. 222) Mk 14:3; Lk 5:29. W. ἐν foll. (Diog. L. 7, 1, 19 ἐν συμποσίῳ) Mk 2:15; Lk 7:37; ἐν εἰδωλείῳ κ. dine in a temple 1 Cor 8:10. On this custom s. New Docs 1, 5–9 w. ins. reff. The sense ‘couch’ for the related noun (κλινή) does not appear in the NT. Cp. Horace, Satires 2, 8 for a Roman couch arrangement at a banquet.—M-M. TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

someth. someth. = something

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

abs. abs. = absolute

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

foll. foll. = followed, following

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

NT NT = New Testament

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 517
